body.text: Node Zero is a sub-$50 BOM hardware prototype designed for autonomic regulation and cognitive sensory gating, specifically targeted at neurodivergent individuals.

Existing assistive-tech vendors sell closed-source devices at medical-device markups to a disproportionately unemployed and uninsured population. P31 Labs releases everything under AGPLv3/MIT and targets hardware under $50 BOM.
85% complete for The Buffer software component
Provides affordable, open-source assistive technology for neurodivergent individuals who are disproportionately unemployed and uninsured.
